visa_types.name: Study
visa_types.documents_required:
Please see the checklist or visit here (https://www.irishimmigration.ie/) for documents required based on the visa you wish to apply.
You are required to submit one(1) set of documents only.
To speed up the time during your submission please do not glue or fasten documents with paper clips or staples.
Please be advised, that all documents in a language different from English must be followed by a notarized translation into English.
Along with your application form you are required to submit all the supporting documents needed.
visa_types.visa_fees:
Some applicants are required to pay a visa fee, while others are exempt. Please refer here (https://www.irishimmigration.ie/) for more information.

[irl > phl > en > Table:Study > Visa Fees 1]
- VFS Service Fees: Php 3152.31
Please be informed that VFS Global Ireland Visa Application center is now.
- Accepting Credit/Debit Cards.
Other payment methods
- Gcash
- Maya
- Alipay
- Shopee Pay
All fees are non-refundable.
Please note that ‘Multiple entry’ visas are only granted by the Embassy of Ireland in limited circumstances. 
To qualify for a Multiple Entry visa:
																		
- Applicants must have held at least two previous single journey visas to Ireland and prove they did not overstay, (i.e. entry/exit stamps in their passports).
- Shows sufficient evidence of need such as tour / cruise itinerary, business itinerary, etc.
If an applicant wrongly applies for a multiple entry visa, we will not refund the difference if a single entry visa is granted. 
Payments will not be refunded if they withdraw their application or if their application is refused.
visa_types.processing_time:
For most short stay visas, please expect a decision within 8-12 weeks due to a current high demand. 
NOTE:
Processing times can change during the year, e.g. before holiday periods.
- Applications are processed in date order. You are advised not to purchase travel tickets before you know the outcome of your visa application.
- Borrowing of passport during the processing period is not permitted. Exceptions and considerations may be made under the discretion of the visa officer for medical emergencies only.
Your application may also take longer if: 
- You have not submitted the necessary supporting documentation
- Your supporting documentation needs to be verified because of personal circumstances.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to come to Ireland to reside for longer than 90 days you can apply for a long stay visa.
All Philippine passport holders require a visa to travel to Ireland.
Citizens of one of seventeen countries covered by the Short-Stay Visa Waiver Programme (https://protect-eu.mimecast.com/s/yjZ_C09Z9fY2WVMuwTTfX?domain=irishimmigration.ie/), may be able to travel to Ireland if they have a valid U.K. C Visa and have recently entered the U.K
Dear Applicants, please submit your complete visa application form and documents required at the Visa Application Centre in person or through an authorized representative.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to come to Ireland to reside for longer than 90 days you can apply for a long stay visa.
All Philippine passport holders require a visa to travel to Ireland.
Citizens of one of seventeen countries covered by the Short-Stay Visa Waiver Programme (https://protect-eu.mimecast.com/s/yjZ_C09Z9fY2WVMuwTTfX?domain=irishimmigration.ie/), may be able to travel to Ireland if they have a valid U.K. C Visa and have recently entered the U.K
Dear Applicants, please submit your complete visa application form and documents required at the Visa Application Centre in person or through an authorized representative.

## Visa Fees
Some applicants are required to pay a visa fee, while others are exempt. Please refer here (https://www.irishimmigration.ie/) for more information.

[irl > phl > en > Table:Study > Visa Fees 1]
- VFS Service Fees: Php 3152.31
Please be informed that VFS Global Ireland Visa Application center is now.
- Accepting Credit/Debit Cards.
Other payment methods
- Gcash
- Maya
- Alipay
- Shopee Pay
All fees are non-refundable.
Please note that ‘Multiple entry’ visas are only granted by the Embassy of Ireland in limited circumstances. 
To qualify for a Multiple Entry visa:
																		
- Applicants must have held at least two previous single journey visas to Ireland and prove they did not overstay, (i.e. entry/exit stamps in their passports).
- Shows sufficient evidence of need such as tour / cruise itinerary, business itinerary, etc.
If an applicant wrongly applies for a multiple entry visa, we will not refund the difference if a single entry visa is granted. 
Payments will not be refunded if they withdraw their application or if their application is refused.

## Documents Required
Please see the checklist or visit here (https://www.irishimmigration.ie/) for documents required based on the visa you wish to apply.
You are required to submit one(1) set of documents only.
To speed up the time during your submission please do not glue or fasten documents with paper clips or staples.
Please be advised, that all documents in a language different from English must be followed by a notarized translation into English.
Along with your application form you are required to submit all the supporting documents needed.

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last three months.
- 2 photo must be submitted.
- Smooth photo paper
- Do not write at the back of the photo
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
For most short stay visas, please expect a decision within 8-12 weeks due to a current high demand. 
NOTE:
Processing times can change during the year, e.g. before holiday periods.
- Applications are processed in date order. You are advised not to purchase travel tickets before you know the outcome of your visa application.
- Borrowing of passport during the processing period is not permitted. Exceptions and considerations may be made under the discretion of the visa officer for medical emergencies only.
Your application may also take longer if: 
- You have not submitted the necessary supporting documentation
- Your supporting documentation needs to be verified because of personal circumstances.

## Online Application Form
The application form for Ireland visa needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form please click here (https://www.visas.inis.gov.ie/avats/OnlineHome.aspx).
An application can only be processed when the on-line form is completed and the required backing documentation, passport photograph and appropriate fee are received by the relevant office as indicated by the on-line system.
It is no longer possible to apply using a downloaded / paper application form.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you would like to visit Ireland for a short period (90 days or less) you will find useful guidelines and details of requirements for your visa applications here.
All Philippine passport holders require a visa to travel to Ireland.
Citizens of one of seventeen countries covered by the Short-Stay Visa Waiver Programme (https://protect-eu.mimecast.com/s/yjZ_C09Z9fY2WVMuwTTfX?domain=irishimmigration.ie/), may be able to travel to Ireland if they have a valid U.K. C Visa and have recently entered the U.K
Dear Applicants, please submit your complete visa application form and documents required at the Visa Application Centre in person or through an authorized representative.

## Visa Fees
Some applicants are required to pay a visa fee, while others are exempt. Please refer here (https://www.irishimmigration.ie/) for more information.

[irl > phl > en > Table:Event > Visa Fees 1]
Please be informed that VFS Global Ireland Visa Application center is now.
- Accepting Credit/Debit Cards.
Other payment methods
- Gcash
- Maya
- Alipay
- Shopee Pay
- VFS Service Fees: Php 3152.31
All fees are non-refundable.
Please note that ‘Multiple entry’ visas are only granted by the Embassy of Ireland in limited circumstances. To qualify for a Multiple Entry visa, applicants must have held at least two previous single journey visas to Ireland and prove they did not overstay, (i.e. entry/exit stamps in their passports). Or shows sufficient evidence of need such as tour / cruise itinerary, business itinerary, etc. If an applicant wrongly applies for a multiple entry visa, we will not refund the difference if a single entry visa is granted. 
Payments will not be refunded if they withdraw their application or if their application is refused.

## Documents Required
Please see the checklist or visit here (https://www.irishimmigration.ie/) for documents required based on the visa you wish to apply.
You are required to submit one(1) set of documents only.
To speed up the time during your submission please do not glue or fasten documents with paper clips or staples.
Please be advised, that all documents in a language different from English must be followed by a notarized translation into English.
Along with your application form you are required to submit all the supporting documents needed.

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last three months.
- 2 photo must be submitted.
- Smooth photo paper
- Do not write at the back of the photo
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
For most short stay visas, please expect a decision within 6-8 weeks from the date their application was received at the visa office, embassy or consulate.
NOTE:
Processing times can change during the year, e.g. before holiday periods.
- Applications are processed in date order. You are advised not to purchase travel tickets before you know the outcome of your visa application.
- Borrowing of passport during the processing period is not permitted. Exceptions and considerations may be made under the discretion of the visa officer for medical emergencies only.
Your application may also take longer if: 
- You have not submitted the necessary supporting documentation
- Your supporting documentation needs to be verified because of personal circumstances.

## Online Application Form
The application form for Ireland visa needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form please click here (https://www.visas.inis.gov.ie/avats/OnlineHome.aspx).
An application can only be processed when the on-line form is completed and the required backing documentation, passport photograph and appropriate fee are received by the relevant office as indicated by the on-line system.
It is no longer possible to apply using a downloaded / paper application form.

## Overview
If you would like to visit Ireland for a short period (90 days or less) you will find useful guidelines and details of requirements for your visa applications here.
All Philippine passport holders require a visa to travel to Ireland.
Citizens of one of seventeen countries covered by the Short-Stay Visa Waiver Programme (https://protect-eu.mimecast.com/s/yjZ_C09Z9fY2WVMuwTTfX?domain=irishimmigration.ie/), may be able to travel to Ireland if they have a valid U.K. C Visa and have recently entered the U.K
Dear Applicants, please submit your complete visa application form and documents required at the Visa Application Centre in person or through an authorized representative.

## Visa Fees
Some applicants are required to pay a visa fee, while others are exempt. Please refer here (https://www.irishimmigration.ie/) for more information.

[irl > phl > en > Table:Conference > Visa Fees 1]
- VFS Service Fees: Php 3152.31
Please be informed that VFS Global Ireland Visa Application center is now.
- Accepting Credit/Debit Cards.
Other payment methods
- Gcash
- Maya
- Alipay
- Shopee Pay
All fees are non-refundable.
Please note that ‘Multiple entry’ visas are only granted by the Embassy of Ireland in limited circumstances. 
To qualify for a Multiple Entry visa:
																		
- Applicants must have held at least two previous single journey visas to Ireland and prove they did not overstay, (i.e. entry/exit stamps in their passports).
- Shows sufficient evidence of need such as tour / cruise itinerary, business itinerary, etc.
If an applicant wrongly applies for a multiple entry visa, we will not refund the difference if a single entry visa is granted. 
Payments will not be refunded if they withdraw their application or if their application is refused.

## Documents Required
Please see the checklist or visit here (https://www.irishimmigration.ie/) for documents required based on the visa you wish to apply.
You are required to submit one(1) set of documents only.
To speed up the time during your submission please do not glue or fasten documents with paper clips or staples.
Please be advised, that all documents in a language different from English must be followed by a notarized translation into English.
Along with your application form you are required to submit all the supporting documents needed.

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last three months.
- 2 photo must be submitted.
- Smooth photo paper
- Do not write at the back of the photo
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
For most short stay visas, please expect a decision within 6-8 weeks from the date their application was received at the visa office, embassy or consulate.
NOTE:
Processing times can change during the year, e.g. before holiday periods.
- Applications are processed in date order. You are advised not to purchase travel tickets before you know the outcome of your visa application.
- Borrowing of passport during the processing period is not permitted. Exceptions and considerations may be made under the discretion of the visa officer for medical emergencies only.
Your application may also take longer if: 
- You have not submitted the necessary supporting documentation
- Your supporting documentation needs to be verified because of personal circumstances.

## Online Application Form
The application form for Ireland visa needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form please click here (https://www.visas.inis.gov.ie/avats/OnlineHome.aspx).
An application can only be processed when the on-line form is completed and the required backing documentation, passport photograph and appropriate fee are received by the relevant office as indicated by the on-line system.
It is no longer possible to apply using a downloaded / paper application form.

## Overview
If you would like to visit Ireland for a short period (90 days or less) you will find useful guidelines and details of requirements for your visa applications here.
All Philippine passport holders require a visa to travel to Ireland.
Citizens of one of seventeen countries covered by the Short-Stay Visa Waiver Programme (https://protect-eu.mimecast.com/s/yjZ_C09Z9fY2WVMuwTTfX?domain=irishimmigration.ie/), may be able to travel to Ireland if they have a valid U.K. C Visa and have recently entered the U.K
Dear Applicants, please submit your complete visa application form and documents required at the Visa Application Centre in person or through an authorized representative.

## Visa Fees
Some applicants are required to pay a visa fee, while others are exempt. Please refer here (https://www.irishimmigration.ie/) for more information.

[irl > phl > en > Table:Visit Family/ Friends > Visa Fees 1]
- VFS Service Fees: Php 3152.31
Please be informed that VFS Global Ireland Visa Application center is now.
- Accepting Credit/Debit Cards.
Other payment methods
- Gcash
- Maya
- Alipay
- Shopee Pay
All fees are non-refundable.
Please note that ‘Multiple entry’ visas are only granted by the Embassy of Ireland in limited circumstances. 
To qualify for a Multiple Entry visa:																	
																		
- Applicants must have held at least two previous single journey visas to Ireland and prove they did not overstay, (i.e. entry/exit stamps in their passports).
- Shows sufficient evidence of need such as tour / cruise itinerary, business itinerary, etc.
If an applicant wrongly applies for a multiple entry visa, we will not refund the difference if a single entry visa is granted. 
Payments will not be refunded if they withdraw their application or if their application is refused.

## Documents Required
Please see the checklist or visit here (https://www.irishimmigration.ie/) for documents required based on the visa you wish to apply.
You are required to submit one(1) set of documents only.
To speed up the time during your submission please do not glue or fasten documents with paper clips or staples.
Please be advised, that all documents in a language different from English must be followed by a notarized translation into English.
Along with your application form you are required to submit all the supporting documents needed.

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last three months.
- 2 photo must be submitted.
- Smooth photo paper
- Do not write at the back of the photo
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
For most short stay visas, please expect a decision within 6-8 weeks from the date their application was received at the visa office, embassy or consulate.
NOTE:
Processing times can change during the year, e.g. before holiday periods.
- Applications are processed in date order. You are advised not to purchase travel tickets before you know the outcome of your visa application.
- Borrowing of passport during the processing period is not permitted. Exceptions and considerations may be made under the discretion of the visa officer for medical emergencies only.
Your application may also take longer if: 
- You have not submitted the necessary supporting documentation
- Your supporting documentation needs to be verified because of personal circumstances.

## Online Application Form
The application form for Ireland visa needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form please click here (https://www.visas.inis.gov.ie/avats/OnlineHome.aspx).
An application can only be processed when the on-line form is completed and the required backing documentation, passport photograph and appropriate fee are received by the relevant office as indicated by the on-line system.
It is no longer possible to apply using a downloaded / paper application form.
